example_prompt: Initialize a Notch Pay payment in XAF and redirect the user to complete via MTN MoMo or Orange Money.

amount.description: Amount to charge. Integer for XAF (no decimals allowed).

email.description: Customer email address. One of email, phone, or customer is required.

phone.description: Customer phone number (e.g. +237XXXXXXXXX). One of email, phone, or customer is required.

oneOf.description: Inline customer object — Notch Pay creates or matches the customer based on these fields.

customer.description: Existing customer ID OR inline customer object. One of email, phone, or customer is required.

reference.description: Your own unique reference for this transaction. Auto-generated if omitted.

callback.description: URL to redirect the user to after payment completion.

locked_currency.description: Force the checkout to only accept this currency (e.g. "XAF"). Prevents the user from selecting a different one in the hosted page.

locked_channel.description: Force the checkout to only accept this channel (e.g. "cm.mtn"). The user cannot pick a different operator.

locked_country.description: Force the checkout to only accept channels available in this ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country (e.g. "CM").

items.description: Optional line items for the order, displayed on the hosted checkout.

shipping.description: Optional shipping information attached to the payment.

customer_meta.description: Free-form metadata stored on the customer record. Stored as-is and returned in webhook payloads.

reference.description: Transaction reference. Store this before redirecting — required to call verify_payment. Format: trx.{id}

callback.description: Callback URL or null if not set.

fees.description: Array of applied fee objects (empty if no fees).

authorization_url.description: URL to redirect the user to complete the payment.

gotchas: Always verify payment status server-side by calling verify_payment (GET /payments/{reference}) before fulfilling an order. The callback redirect can be forged by the user.
The transaction object has no 'id' field — use 'reference' (format: trx.xxx) as the unique identifier. The 'sandbox' field is an integer (1 = sandbox, 0 = live), not a boolean.
Use a unique, idempotent reference per payment. Reusing a reference across different transactions will cause reconciliation errors.
The Authorization header value is your public key with no prefix — do not add 'Bearer ' before it.
For sandbox testing, use a public key containing 'test_' in its value. The same API endpoint is used for both test and live keys.
At least one of email, phone, or customer is required. Omitting all three returns a 422 error.

The currency must match the channel's country: XAF for cm.* channels (Cameroon), XOF for ci.*/sn.* (Côte d'Ivoire/Senegal), KES for ke.* (Kenya), UGX for ug.* (Uganda), GHS for gh.* (Ghana). A mismatch between currency and channel returns 422.
